info.title: Unified Mock Enterprise Services
info.description:
Combined mock systems simulating WorkWeek and ServiceImmediately.

This portal serves as a target ecosystem for multi-agent A-to-A testing. It exposes standard REST endpoints for the client UI alongside mounted stateless Streamable HTTP MCP servers.

## Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration

The application mounts two FastMCP servers as sub-applications, exposing tools and resources via the stateless Streamable HTTP transport protocol.

### 1. MCP Server Endpoints & Capabilities

#### 📅 WorkWeek Server (`/work-week/mcp/`)
Exposes employee profiles and leave management workflows.
* **Resources**:
  * `workweek://employees/{employee_id}/profile`: Returns employee metadata (name, email, role, home_address, phone_number, manager_id).
  * `workweek://employees/{employee_id}/timeoff`: Returns raw database leave balances (accrued and used vacation/sick days).
* **Tools**:
  * `get_current_employee_id()`: Resolves employee ID of the authenticated user session.
  * `get_employee_balances(employee_id)`: Fetches remaining vacation and sick leave balances.
  * `request_time_off(employee_id, start_date, end_date, leave_type, days)`: Books vacation/sick leave. Dates must be `YYYY-MM-DD`. Start date cannot be in the past or after the end date. Employee must have sufficient remaining balances.
  * `update_personal_info(employee_id, address, phone)`: Updates home address (min 5 chars) and phone number (must match regex `^\+?[\d\s\-()]{7,20}$`).

#### 🎫 ServiceImmediately Server (`/service-immediately/mcp/`)
Mock ITSM/HRSD ticket tracking system.
* **Resources**:
  * `serviceimmediately://tickets/{ticket_id}`: Returns ticket details, current status, assignment details, and comment timelines.
* **Tools**:
  * `list_tickets(employee_id)`: Lists all incident tickets requested by a specific employee (requires caller context ownership verification).
  * `create_ticket(requested_by, category, short_description, priority, assignment_group='Service Desk')`: Submits a new incident. Rejects duplicate submissions within 5 minutes. Critical priority (priority='1 - Critical') tickets must describe an active outage, crash, or system downtime keyword.
  * `add_ticket_comment(ticket_id, author, comment)`: Appends a timeline comment to the ticket's activity log.
  * `update_ticket_status(ticket_id, status, resolution_notes='', updated_by='System')`: Drives the ticket status state machine. Valid transitions: `New -> In Progress/Closed`, `In Progress -> Resolved/Closed`, `Resolved -> In Progress/Closed`. Closed tickets are locked and cannot transition anymore.

#### 🔒 Tenant Isolation Rules
To enforce data privacy, every resource query and tool execution executes strict identity context verification. Authenticated sessions can only act on resources and profiles corresponding to their own employee ID context, otherwise returning access denied values.

### 2. Authentication (Production)
In production, endpoints bypass Identity-Aware Proxy (IAP) but require a **Personal Access Token (PAT)** for authentication.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Due to Google Frontend (GFE) intercepting and validating standard `Authorization` headers, you **must** pass the token in the custom `X-MCP-Token` header:

  ```http
  X-MCP-Token: mcp_your_token_here
  ```

### 3. ADK Agent Configurations

#### Option A: Connection using Custom Headers (X-MCP-Token)
```python
from google.adk.agents import Agent
from google.adk.tools.mcp_tool import McpToolset
from google.adk.tools.mcp_tool.mcp_session_manager import StreamableHTTPConnectionParams

# Connect to the WorkWeek MCP server statelessly
workweek_mcp = McpToolset(
    connection_params=StreamableHTTPConnectionParams(
        url="https://mock-saas.aishprabhat.demo.altostrat.com/work-week/mcp/",
        headers={"X-MCP-Token": "mcp_your_token_here"}
    )
)

# Connect to the ServiceImmediately MCP server statelessly
serviceimmediately_mcp = McpToolset(
    connection_params=StreamableHTTPConnectionParams(
        url="https://mock-saas.aishprabhat.demo.altostrat.com/service-immediately/mcp/",
        headers={"X-MCP-Token": "mcp_your_token_here"}
    )
)

enterprise_agent = Agent(
    name="enterprise_agent",
    model="gemini-3.5-flash",
    description="Enterprise assistant managing WorkWeek profiles and IT support tickets.",
    instruction="Assist the user using tools from the mock enterprise systems.",
    tools=[workweek_mcp, serviceimmediately_mcp],
)
```

#### Option B: Standard Python MCP Client
If you are writing a custom Python tool or agent using the official `mcp` SDK, connect to the Streamable HTTP server as follows:
```python
import asyncio
import httpx
from mcp import ClientSession
from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamable_http_client

async def main():
    mcp_url = "https://mock-saas.aishprabhat.demo.altostrat.com/work-week/mcp/"
    headers = {"X-MCP-Token": "mcp_your_token_here"}

    async with httpx.AsyncClient(headers=headers) as client:
        async with streamable_http_client(mcp_url, http_client=client) as (read, write, get_session_id):
            async with ClientSession(read, write) as session:
                await session.initialize()
                print("Session initialized!")
                # List available tools
                tools_response = await session.list_tools()
                for tool in tools_response.tools:
                    print(f"Tool discovered: {tool.name}")

asyncio.run(main())
```

#### Option C: REST API Endpoints
You can also invoke the standard REST API endpoints directly. In production, these require an identity header provided by Identity-Aware Proxy (IAP):
```bash
# Get the profile details of the current logged-in employee
curl -H "x-goog-authenticated-user-email: aishprabhat@google.com" \
  https://mock-saas.aishprabhat.demo.altostrat.com/work-week/api/employees/current/profile
```

### 4. Testing Connection
```bash
venv/bin/python tests/mcp_client_check.py https://mock-saas.aishprabhat.demo.altostrat.com/work-week/mcp/ <token>
```

get.description: Retrieves detailed personal work and contact metadata for an employee.

post.description: Updates the editable personal fields (home address and phone number) of the employee.

get.description: Fetches vacation and sick balances including days accrued, used, and remaining.

post.description: Submits a leave request. Deducts balances if the remaining days are sufficient.

get.description: Retrieves feedback messages provided to the employee.
Fetches the history of all requested time off.

put.description: Updates an existing leave request and adjusts employee used balances accordingly.

delete.description: Deletes a leave request and refunds the days back to the employee's balance.

get.description: Retrieves a list of all incidents matching the logged-in user or active list.

post.description: Creates a new ServiceImmediately incident record and assigns it to default groups.

get.description: Retrieves structural details for a specific incident including comments timeline.

post.description: Appends a custom post comment onto the incident's Activity log timeline.
Modifies the lifecycle state of the ticket (In Progress, Resolved) and adds updates.
